description:
Explorer thumbnails for 313 file types Windows can't read, plus a right-click image toolkit.

SageThumbs 2K is a modern, crash-isolated Windows 11 shell extension written in Rust that rebuilds the classic SageThumbs. It gives File Explorer thumbnails for 313 file types Windows can't preview on its own — camera RAW, HEIC/AVIF, Photoshop, DjVu, ebooks and comics, video frames, 3D-print files and a long tail of obscure formats — plus a right-click image toolkit (convert, resize, rotate, OCR, color picker, metadata strip, combine-to-PDF/CBZ) and a console/AI CLI. Free for personal use.
SageThumbs 2K 0.8.0 — Sync your settings across your PCs
✨ New: optional settings sync
Settings has a new Data & Backup section with a "Sync settings…" button. Sign in with a Connections account (it opens your real browser — SageThumbs never sees your password) and your portable preferences follow you to every machine you sign into: thumbnail limits & quality, the right-click menu layout & toggles, hotkeys, language, and ebook/comic options.
- Off by default — no network happens unless you turn it on.
- Privacy-first — only an allowlist of portable settings syncs; never your files, folder paths, passwords, or images. Your settings always stay on your PC too, so everything keeps working fully offline or signed out. Disconnect anytime and the cloud copy is removed.
- Secure sign-in — the token that keeps you signed in is encrypted on your machine with Windows' DPAPI and is never part of the synced data.
- Still crash-isolated — all sign-in/sync code lives in the Settings app; the thumbnail shell extension itself never touches the network.
Also included (rolled up from the never-tagged 0.7.2)
Hotkeys now survive sleep / lock / updates, the tray icon survives Explorer restarts, more reliable screenshot copying, OCR bindable to your custom hotkey, much faster "Sort into folders ▸ By image size", capture & upload progress feedback, and several st2k CLI fixes. See the full changelog.
Install: download SageThumbs2K-Setup-0.8.0.exe below and run it. Thumbnails for 316 file types Windows can't show on its own, a right-click image toolkit, and now settings that follow you across PCs.
